pattern: Sorting whole rows by a single fixed column reduces to an ordinary comparison sort keyed on that column.
hints: Only one column (the kth) decides the order; the rest of each row just rides along.
Sort the rows themselves (not just the column) using each row's value at index k as the sort key, descending.
Because the scores in that column are guaranteed distinct, a straightforward descending sort on that key gives a single, unambiguous order.

edge_inputs.description: three students, sort by the middle exam
two students, sort by the first exam
sort by the last exam column
rows already sorted descending by the key column
rows sorted ascending by the key column, needing a full reversal
a wide matrix with more exams than students
values at the maximum allowed score

stress.description: a large matrix near the maximum size with distinct scores in the key column
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, min(size, 250))
    n = max(1, min(size, 250))
    k = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    key_values = list(range(1, m + 1))
    rng.shuffle(key_values)
    matrix = []
    for i in range(m):
        row = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
        row[k] = key_values[i]
        matrix.append(row)
    return [matrix, k]
